content:
Summary: Updated the CSV spreadsheet viewer so every row-number cell includes a top border, not just the first row. The change kept the existing sticky bottom horizontal scrollbar and all CSV parsing/data flow intact, and was limited to the ArtifactViewer presentation layer.

Decisions:
1. [finished] The border adjustment was kept local to SpreadsheetViewer to avoid changing shared CSV ingest or artifact mapping logic.
2. [finished] The table border styling now applies uniformly to the row-number column instead of conditionally only on the first row.

Affected files:
1. [modified] src/components/ArtifactViewer/SpreadsheetViewer.tsx — Applied a top border to every row-number cell so the CSV table grid remains consistent across all rows. | Notes: This is the only file changed in the follow-up fix.

content:
Summary: Added the missing top border back to the CSV spreadsheet viewer's row-number column. The header number cell now includes a top border, and the first data-row number cell also receives a top border so the leftmost column edge reads correctly without altering the existing sticky bottom scrollbar or CSV parsing behavior.

Decisions:
1. [finished] The border fix was kept local to SpreadsheetViewer to avoid touching CSV ingest or the shared data model.
2. [finished] The top border was applied only where needed rather than adding extra borders to every cell, to preserve the table's visual consistency.

Affected files:
1. [modified] src/components/ArtifactViewer/SpreadsheetViewer.tsx — Restored the top border on the row-number column without changing the CSV data flow or scrollbar behavior. | Notes: This is the only file changed for the top-border fix.

content:
Summary: Refined the CSV spreadsheet viewer after initial table support landed. The row-number column now includes a left border so its cell edge matches the rest of the table, while the sticky bottom horizontal scrollbar arrangement remains in place for easier sideways scrolling. This change was limited to the ArtifactViewer spreadsheet component and did not alter CSV parsing or ingest behavior.

Decisions:
1. [finished] The row-number column uses the same border styling as the rest of the table to avoid a visually thicker or missing edge.
2. [finished] The horizontal scrollbar implementation stays plain and bottom-aligned to match the user's requested behavior.

Affected files:
1. [modified] src/components/ArtifactViewer/SpreadsheetViewer.tsx — Added the missing left border to the row-number cells and preserved the sticky bottom scrollbar layout. | Notes: This is the only file changed in this follow-up fix.

metadata.summary: Refined the CSV spreadsheet viewer after initial table support landed. The row-number column now includes a left border so its cell edge matches the rest of the table, while the sticky bottom horizontal scrollbar arrangement remains in place for easier sideways scrolling. This change was limited to the ArtifactViewer spreadsheet component and did not alter CSV parsing or ingest behavior.

content:
Summary: Implemented CSV-to-table support for workspace uploads. CSV files now flow through a shared CSV parser/serializer at ingest time, are stored as canonical row text, and are mapped to the spreadsheet artifact viewer so they render as rows and columns instead of plain document lines. The existing line-based search and highlight flow remains intact because CSV rows are still persisted as line-oriented content.

Decisions:
1. [finished] CSV parsing and serialization were kept in a shared lib module so ingest and the client-side spreadsheet viewer use the same rules.
2. [finished] Embedded newlines in CSV cells are flattened during canonicalization to preserve the existing one-line-per-row workspace model.

Affected files:
1. [created] src/lib/workspace-files/csv.ts — Provides shared CSV parsing and serialization for both server-side ingest and client-side spreadsheet rendering. | Notes: Contains the canonical row parser/serializer used by the new CSV table flow.
2. [modified] src/lib/workspace-files/ingest.ts — Uses the shared CSV helper to parse CSV uploads into canonical row text before storing extracted lines. | Notes: CSV now gets row-aware preprocessing while other file types keep their existing extraction paths.
3. [modified] src/lib/workspace-files/mappers.ts — Routes CSV workspace files to the sheet artifact type so the spreadsheet viewer is used. | Notes: Document files still use the text viewer.
4. [modified] src/components/ArtifactViewer/SpreadsheetViewer.tsx — Parses CSV content with the shared helper to render accurate rows and columns instead of splitting by commas naively. | Notes: Keeps the existing line-number/highlight behavior.

metadata.summary: Implemented CSV-to-table support for workspace uploads. CSV files now flow through a shared CSV parser/serializer at ingest time, are stored as canonical row text, and are mapped to the spreadsheet artifact viewer so they render as rows and columns instead of plain document lines. The existing line-based search and highlight flow remains intact because CSV rows are still persisted as line-oriented content.

content:
Summary: Implemented CSV support in the workspace file upload flow. The upload validation allowlist, accept attribute, and user-facing labels now include CSV, and CSV is treated as a supported text-like upload without a separate parser. This was kept as a narrow change to the shared workspace-file boundary and the small UI copy surfaces that mention supported formats.

Decisions:
1. [finished] CSV support was added by updating the shared workspace-file validation source of truth instead of duplicating allowlist logic in the UI.
2. [finished] CSV content is currently ingested as text rather than structured rows, which matches the existing line-based workspace extraction model.

Affected files:
1. [modified] src/lib/workspace-files/validation.ts — Expanded the canonical supported file list and accept/mime mapping to include CSV uploads. | Notes: Shared source of truth for both client and server upload validation.
2. [modified] src/lib/workspace-files/ingest.ts — Updated the unsupported-file error message so it matches the new CSV-capable upload list. | Notes: CSV continues to use the existing plain-text extraction path.
3. [modified] src/components/ArtifactViewer/EmptyWorkspace.tsx — Updated empty-state guidance so users see CSV listed among supported upload types. | Notes: Copy only; no behavioral changes.
4. [modified] src/components/ArtifactViewer/WorkspacePanelHeader.tsx — Updated the panel subtitle to keep the displayed supported file types accurate. | Notes: Copy only; no behavioral changes.
5. [modified] src/components/UploadModal/index.tsx — Updated the displayed format chips so the upload modal reflects CSV support. | Notes: The input already consumed the shared accept string, so no separate behavior change was needed here.

The upload validation allowlist, accept attribute, and user-facing labels now include CSV, and CSV is treated as a supported text-like upload without a separate parser. This was kept as a narrow change to the shared workspace-file boundary and the small UI copy surfaces that mention supported formats.

content:
Summary: Updated the workspace page modal flow so Upload files now chains through workspace creation when no workspace exists. The page tracks a pending upload intent, opens the Create Workspace modal when needed, clears the intent on cancel, and automatically opens the Upload modal after a successful workspace creation. The change stayed localized to src/app/page.tsx and reused existing modal components.

Decisions:
1. [finished] Keep the orchestration in src/app/page.tsx rather than adding new modal components or cross-component coupling, because the behavior is a simple page-level flow between existing modals.
2. [finished] Clear the pending upload intent when the create-workspace modal is canceled to avoid surprising automatic uploads on future workspace creation.

Affected files:
1. [modified] src/app/page.tsx — This file owns the workspace page state and modal orchestration, so it was the correct place to add the create-then-upload handoff. | Notes: Added a pending upload flag, a close handler for the create modal, and automatic upload modal opening after workspace creation.
2. [inspected] src/components/CreateWorkspaceModal/index.tsx — The existing create workspace modal needed to be reused as-is, so its close and submit behavior was checked before wiring the new flow. | Notes: No code changes were required.
3. [inspected] src/components/UploadModal/index.tsx — The upload modal behavior was reviewed to ensure it could still open directly after workspace creation without needing component changes. | Notes: No code changes were required.

The page tracks a pending upload intent, opens the Create Workspace modal when needed, clears the intent on cancel, and automatically opens the Upload modal after a successful workspace creation. The change stayed localized to src/app/page.tsx and reused existing modal components.

content:
Summary: Fixed a Next.js production build failure caused by outdated workspace repository calls in workspace API routes. The chat and conversations workspace routes now fetch the authenticated user ID before calling findWorkspaceById, matching the repository signature and preserving user scoping. This addressed the TypeScript build error and aligned the route behavior with existing authorization patterns.

Decisions:
1. [finished] Keep workspace lookups scoped to the authenticated user in API routes instead of loosening the repository contract or making the helper optional.
2. [finished] Fix all discovered one-argument call sites for findWorkspaceById in the affected workspace routes rather than patching only the single build error line.

Affected files:
1. [modified] src/app/api/workspaces/[workspaceId]/chat/route.ts — This route caused the reported TypeScript build failure and now enforces authenticated workspace lookup. | Notes: Added user authentication guard and passed userId to findWorkspaceById.
2. [modified] src/app/api/workspaces/[workspaceId]/conversations/route.ts — This route used the same outdated repository call signature and would also fail type checking. | Notes: Added user authentication guard and passed userId to findWorkspaceById.
3. [inspected] src/lib/workspace/repository.ts — The helper signature defined the contract that the routes had to satisfy. | Notes: No code change was needed, only verification of the two-argument signature.

metadata.summary: Fixed a Next.js production build failure caused by outdated workspace repository calls in workspace API routes. The chat and conversations workspace routes now fetch the authenticated user ID before calling findWorkspaceById, matching the repository signature and preserving user scoping. This addressed the TypeScript build error and aligned the route behavior with existing authorization patterns.

content:
Summary: Implemented the chat thread ordering fix so streamed assistant/tool content now renders top-to-bottom in chronological order. The live assistant stream in useChat now splits into separate assistant segments around tool calls, preventing post-tool deltas from being appended to the pre-tool placeholder bubble. The task card was moved to done after the change was applied.

Decisions:
1. [finished] Preserved the existing oldest-first history order from the workspace chat API and changed only client-side streaming segmentation, because the visual bug was caused by live message reuse across tool boundaries.
2. [finished] Chose to append a fresh assistant segment after tool calls rather than trying to reorder existing bubbles, which keeps the message thread chronologically correct and minimizes risk to persisted history rendering.
3. [finished] Added a small empty-delta guard so blank stream chunks do not create empty assistant bubbles after a tool boundary.

Affected files:
1. [modified] src/lib/chat/useChat.ts — Implemented the streaming message segmentation fix so assistant output is split around tool calls and rendered chronologically in the message list. | Notes: Added active assistant message tracking, tool-boundary placeholder removal, new assistant segment creation after tool calls, and safer error fallback handling.

metadata.summary: Implemented the chat thread ordering fix so streamed assistant/tool content now renders top-to-bottom in chronological order. The live assistant stream in useChat now splits into separate assistant segments around tool calls, preventing post-tool deltas from being appended to the pre-tool placeholder bubble. The task card was moved to done after the change was applied.

content:
Summary: Implemented workspace-aware AI tools for the OpenAI SDK chat flow. The backend now exposes four server-side workspace tools (list artifacts, global search, keyword location, and line-range reads) backed by the Supabase workspace file repository, and the chat route streams tool events plus assistant deltas through SSE. The client now renders tool call cards with stable ids and can jump to artifacts/ranges from tool invocations.

Decisions:
1. [finished] Bind workspace access server-side inside the tool layer instead of requiring the model to pass workspaceId through every tool call, to reduce cross-workspace risk and keep the route as the source of truth.
2. [finished] Keep tool execution results as JSON strings for model consumption, while emitting compact SSE events for the UI instead of pushing raw tool payloads into the message thread.
3. [finished] Use the existing chat/completions OpenAI SDK path rather than switching to a different agent framework, because the repository already streams chat through OpenAI and only needed tool support added to that flow.

Affected files:
1. [created] src/lib/workspace-files/search.ts — Added shared workspace file search and read helpers that power all four AI tools without duplicating repository logic. | Notes: Includes listing, global search, keyword location, and line-range extraction helpers.
2. [created] src/lib/ai/workspace-tools.ts — Added the OpenAI tool schemas and workspace-bound invocation preparation layer required for model tool calling. | Notes: Validates tool arguments with Zod and returns structured descriptors for UI events.
3. [modified] src/lib/chat/openai.ts — Replaced the old unused OpenAI helper with the new tool-aware chat orchestration flow using the OpenAI SDK. | Notes: Handles streamed deltas, tool call loops, and tool result messages.
4. [modified] src/app/api/chat/route.ts — Wired the HTTP chat endpoint to the new OpenAI tool orchestration and SSE event stream for tool calls and tool results. | Notes: Still persists user and assistant messages to Supabase.
5. [modified] src/lib/chat/useChat.ts — Updated the client chat hook to render tool call lifecycle events and keep tool statuses synchronized in the conversation thread. | Notes: Now tracks tool call ids from the SSE stream.
6. [modified] src/components/CommandCenter/types.ts — Expanded the tool call type so client state can identify and update individual tool invocations. | Notes: Added artifactId to support document navigation from tool cards.
7. [modified] src/components/CommandCenter/MessageBubble.tsx — Adjusted the live-message rendering so completed tool call cards do not keep showing the streaming loading indicator. | Notes: Only plain assistant placeholders now render the thinking dots.
8. [modified] src/app/page.tsx — Updated tool call navigation to resolve artifacts by id first and then by name, enabling precise file jumps from tool cards. | Notes: Works with read/range tool results.
9. [modified] package.json — Declared zod as a direct dependency because the new tool layer uses Zod schemas at runtime and type validation boundaries. | Notes: Version added matches the lockfile entry already present in the workspace.
10. [modified] package-lock.json — Kept the root dependency manifest aligned with package.json after adding the direct zod dependency. | Notes: Only the root dependency list was updated; the existing zod package entry already existed in the lockfile.

metadata.summary: Implemented workspace-aware AI tools for the OpenAI SDK chat flow. The backend now exposes four server-side workspace tools (list artifacts, global search, keyword location, and line-range reads) backed by the Supabase workspace file repository, and the chat route streams tool events plus assistant deltas through SSE. The client now renders tool call cards with stable ids and can jump to artifacts/ranges from tool invocations.

content:
Summary: Updated workspace document ingestion so extracted PDF/DOCX/TXT/MD content now drops leading and trailing blank lines before storing both the numbered line array and the concatenated extracted text. This keeps the downstream contract intact while removing empty boundary lines from uploaded documents.

Decisions:
1. [finished] Keep boundary-line cleanup inside the ingestion normalization layer instead of changing the workspace viewer or API contract.
2. [finished] Preserve internal blank lines and only remove empty lines at the top and bottom to avoid altering document structure more than necessary.

Affected files:
1. [modified] src/lib/workspace-files/ingest.ts — Implemented trimming of leading and trailing empty lines during workspace document ingestion and rebuilt stored extracted text from the trimmed lines. | Notes: No other files were changed for this fix.

metadata.summary: Updated workspace document ingestion so extracted PDF/DOCX/TXT/MD content now drops leading and trailing blank lines before storing both the numbered line array and the concatenated extracted text. This keeps the downstream contract intact while removing empty boundary lines from uploaded documents.

content:
Summary: Switched workspace file ingestion from hand-rolled parsing to dedicated PDF/DOCX libraries. The upload path now targets @cedrugs/pdf-parse for PDFs and mammoth for DOCX, while continuing to normalize extracted text into numbered lines for the viewer and future AI file-reading tool. Added ambient declarations for the new modules and updated package.json dependencies accordingly.

Decisions:
1. [finished] Use maintained document-parsing libraries rather than custom PDF/DOCX heuristics because the heuristics were producing unreadable Unicode-like output for real documents.

Affected files:
1. [modified] src/lib/workspace-files/ingest.ts — Replaced the custom PDF/DOCX extraction heuristics with proper parser-based extraction so uploads yield readable text lines. | Notes: Keeps sanitization and line normalization after extraction.
2. [modified] package.json — Added the new parser dependencies required for readable PDF and DOCX extraction. | Notes: Dependencies need to be installed into node_modules.
3. [created] src/types/mammoth.d.ts — Provides an explicit TypeScript contract for the mammoth parser import used by the ingestion layer. | Notes: Keeps the module boundary typed.
4. [created] src/types/cedrugs-pdf-parse.d.ts — Provides an explicit TypeScript contract for the PDF parser import used by the ingestion layer. | Notes: Keeps the module boundary typed.

metadata.summary: Switched workspace file ingestion from hand-rolled parsing to dedicated PDF/DOCX libraries. The upload path now targets @cedrugs/pdf-parse for PDFs and mammoth for DOCX, while continuing to normalize extracted text into numbered lines for the viewer and future AI file-reading tool. Added ambient declarations for the new modules and updated package.json dependencies accordingly.

content:
Summary: Fixed the workspace file upload failure caused by malformed Unicode reaching Supabase. The ingestion pipeline now sanitizes extracted and inserted text to remove NULs and lone surrogates before persistence, which should prevent the Postgres Unicode escape error during file uploads.

Decisions:
1. [finished] Sanitize Unicode at both the ingestion and repository boundaries so future file parsers cannot accidentally persist invalid text to Supabase.

Affected files:
1. [modified] src/lib/workspace-files/ingest.ts — Added Unicode normalization and sanitization for extracted file text, line arrays, and upload metadata before persistence. | Notes: Removes NUL characters and lone surrogates.
2. [modified] src/lib/workspace-files/repository.ts — Added defensive sanitization before the Supabase insert to prevent malformed Unicode from reaching the database. | Notes: Provides a second validation layer at persistence time.

Root memory: 144113e3-18c7-413e-a408-87aa6c726015

Related memories: 0fbd4f84-34d9-4153-ab3b-a069ca465fa3, f711082b-2766-4a8a-9e95-dd15d29678fa, 6a80611f-a231-44c2-8daa-8e5b092fbaa6, eb4f7a09-29ce-450f-b61a-85e2067333ee

metadata.summary: Fixed the workspace file upload failure caused by malformed Unicode reaching Supabase. The ingestion pipeline now sanitizes extracted and inserted text to remove NULs and lone surrogates before persistence, which should prevent the Postgres Unicode escape error during file uploads.

content:
Summary: Implemented workspace file upload and text extraction flow. The workspace panel now exposes a real upload button and empty-state CTA, uploads txt/md/pdf/docx through a workspace-scoped API route, extracts normalized line-based text on the server, persists uploaded file metadata and extracted lines in Supabase, and keeps a repository hook ready for future AI file-reading tools.

Decisions:
1. [finished] Use a dedicated workspace_files table that stores normalized text and line arrays instead of raw binary storage, so future AI tools can read the same source of truth directly from Supabase.
2. [finished] Keep the upload UX inside the left workspace panel with a header upload button and empty-state upload CTA, while leaving the page as the orchestration layer.
3. [finished] Implement pdf and docx extraction without new npm dependencies by using best-effort server-side parsers, to keep the feature self-contained in this change.

Affected files:
1. [created] sqls/002_workspace_files.sql — Adds the new Supabase table and related indexes/triggers needed to persist uploaded files and extracted lines. | Notes: Includes RLS enablement and updated_at trigger.
2. [modified] src/lib/supabase/types.ts — Extends the generated-style database contract with workspace file row, insert, and relationship types. | Notes: Keeps client/server type usage explicit.
3. [created] src/lib/workspace-files/validation.ts — Centralizes file-type allowlisting, size limits, and accept-string generation for upload validation. | Notes: Shared by client and server code.
4. [created] src/lib/workspace-files/ingest.ts — Implements server-side extraction and normalization for txt, md, pdf, and docx files into line arrays. | Notes: Uses built-in parsing logic rather than external packages.
5. [created] src/lib/workspace-files/repository.ts — Provides Supabase persistence and retrieval helpers for uploaded workspace files, plus a reusable line-range helper for future AI reads. | Notes: Server-side data access only.
6. [created] src/lib/workspace-files/client.ts — Wraps the workspace file API for the client-side page orchestration layer. | Notes: Handles fetch, upload, and response validation.
7. [created] src/lib/workspace-files/mappers.ts — Converts persisted file rows into ArtifactViewer artifacts so uploaded files render with the existing line-based viewer. | Notes: Keeps UI mapping separate from data access.
8. [created] src/app/api/workspaces/[workspaceId]/files/route.ts — Exposes workspace-scoped GET and POST endpoints for listing and uploading extracted files. | Notes: Validates workspace existence and multipart uploads.
9. [modified] src/components/ArtifactViewer/index.tsx — Adds the workspace file header upload control to the left panel while keeping the viewer composition-focused. | Notes: Now accepts fileCount and onUploadFiles props.
10. [modified] src/components/ArtifactViewer/EmptyWorkspace.tsx — Reworks the empty state into a file upload call to action with the supported document formats. | Notes: Removed the old sample deploy wording.
11. [created] src/components/ArtifactViewer/WorkspacePanelHeader.tsx — Provides the new top-of-panel upload entry point and file count display without bloating the viewer entry file. | Notes: Uses neutral styling consistent with the app.
12. [modified] src/app/page.tsx — Orchestrates workspace file loading, upload actions, hidden file input handling, and artifact state synchronization. | Notes: Keeps the page as the composition layer and delegates extraction/persistence to helpers.

metadata.summary: Implemented workspace file upload and text extraction flow. The workspace panel now exposes a real upload button and empty-state CTA, uploads txt/md/pdf/docx through a workspace-scoped API route, extracts normalized line-based text on the server, persists uploaded file metadata and extracted lines in Supabase, and keeps a repository hook ready for future AI file-reading tools.

content:
Summary: User wants the left workspace panel (ArtifactViewer area) to support uploading files, extract text from txt/md/pdf/docx into line-based content, persist the extracted content in Supabase, and leave a clean hook for a future AI file-reading tool. I inspected the workspace page, ArtifactViewer, chat input, workspace repository, Supabase schema/types, and existing API patterns to shape the plan.

Decisions:
1. [finished] Treat the left ArtifactViewer workspace panel as the place to integrate file upload and document previews, rather than building a separate top-level upload screen.
2. [finished] Use a server-side ingestion flow with validated file-type allowlisting and normalized line extraction so the same source of truth can later power an AI reading tool.
3. [finished] Prefer storing extracted text/lines and file metadata in Supabase first; defer raw binary storage unless a future requirement needs original file downloads.

Affected files:
1. [inspected] src/app/page.tsx — This is the top-level workspace composition and controls the left ArtifactViewer panel where upload support should be integrated. | Notes: Contains current workspace state, sample artifacts, and panel layout.
2. [inspected] src/components/ArtifactViewer/index.tsx — This is the workspace panel component that will likely host upload/dropzone and file preview behavior. | Notes: Currently only renders tabs, content, and an empty state CTA.
3. [inspected] src/components/ArtifactViewer/EmptyWorkspace.tsx — This empty state is the most likely UI entry point for adding upload affordances and supported-format guidance. | Notes: Currently only offers a sample deploy button.
4. [inspected] src/components/CommandCenter/ChatInput.tsx — Contains an existing attachment button that may inform or share UX patterns for file input styling. | Notes: No file upload logic exists yet.
5. [inspected] src/lib/workspace/repository.ts — Shows the established Supabase repository pattern to mirror for workspace file persistence. | Notes: Useful for future repository separation.
6. [inspected] src/lib/chat/repository.ts — Provides the repository structure and validation/error-handling style to follow for new file ingestion helpers. | Notes: Good reference for server-side data access conventions.
7. [inspected] src/lib/supabase/types.ts — This file will need to be extended to include any new file/document tables introduced by the migration. | Notes: Must stay in sync with Supabase schema.
8. [inspected] sqls/001_initial_schema.sql — Baseline migration shows current tables and trigger/RLS patterns to extend for uploaded files. | Notes: New migration should follow the same style.
9. [inspected] src/app/api/workspaces/route.ts — Defines the existing API style for workspace list/create routes, which new file routes should match. | Notes: Validates JSON handling and error responses.
10. [inspected] src/app/api/workspaces/[workspaceId]/chat/route.ts — Shows the pattern for workspace-scoped API routes that the file ingestion route should mirror. | Notes: Useful for params handling and 404 behavior.

I inspected the workspace page, ArtifactViewer, chat input, workspace repository, Supabase schema/types, and existing API patterns to shape the plan.

content:
Summary: Implemented a Supabase-backed workspace and chat flow. The app now loads real workspaces from Supabase, allows creating a workspace through a modal opened from a plus button in the sidebar and mobile header, and loads workspace-specific chat history through a workspace chat API. The chat send path now requires a valid workspace, validates that conversations belong to the selected workspace, and falls back to creating a conversation inside that workspace when needed. No SQL migration changes were required because the existing workspaces/conversations/messages schema already supported the flow.

Decisions:
1. [finished] Keep the page as the orchestrator and split persistence concerns into small API routes and repository helpers rather than embedding Supabase logic inside the page component.
2. [finished] Use a workspace-level active chat loader that fetches the latest conversation/messages for the selected workspace instead of storing mocked per-workspace chat state in the UI.
3. [finished] Require a valid workspace for chat submissions instead of auto-creating a default workspace in the chat route, so users are forced into explicit workspace creation.

Affected files:
1. [modified] src/app/page.tsx — Replaced mock workspace state with Supabase-loaded workspaces, added workspace creation flow, and wired the page to the new chat loader and modal. | Notes: Main UI orchestrator for the feature.
2. [modified] src/components/Sidebar.tsx — Sidebar now shows real workspace rows, adds a plus button for creating workspaces, and handles the empty-workspace state. | Notes: Desktop navigation entrypoint.
3. [created] src/components/CreateWorkspaceModal/index.tsx — New modal component for creating workspaces from the sidebar/mobile header CTA with validation and inline error handling. | Notes: Keeps workspace creation UI isolated.
4. [modified] src/lib/chat/useChat.ts — Hook now loads persisted workspace chat history from Supabase before streaming new messages and blocks sends while history is loading. | Notes: Client-side chat state and streaming logic.
5. [created] src/app/api/workspaces/route.ts — New API route to list workspaces and create a new workspace with validation. | Notes: Server boundary for workspace CRUD.
6. [created] src/app/api/workspaces/[workspaceId]/chat/route.ts — New API route to return the latest conversation and persisted messages for a workspace. | Notes: Server boundary for workspace-specific chat loading.
7. [modified] src/app/api/chat/route.ts — Chat streaming route now enforces valid workspace ownership for conversations and no longer falls back to the old auto-created default workspace flow. | Notes: Prevents chats from escaping the selected workspace.
8. [modified] src/lib/workspace/repository.ts — Added a listWorkspaces helper used by the new workspace listing API. | Notes: Keeps workspace data access centralized.
9. [modified] src/lib/chat/repository.ts — Added workspace conversation listing helpers used by the workspace chat loader. | Notes: Supports selecting the active workspace conversation.
10. [modified] src/components/CommandCenter/index.tsx — Added a neutral empty-state panel so the chat area still has a useful workspace-ready state when there are no messages. | Notes: Improves UX for newly created or empty workspaces.
11. [inspected] sqls/001_initial_schema.sql — Verified the existing schema already contained workspaces, conversations, and messages, so no migration was needed for this feature. | Notes: No file changes required.

The chat send path now requires a valid workspace, validates that conversations belong to the selected workspace, and falls back to creating a conversation inside that workspace when needed. No SQL migration changes were required because the existing workspaces/conversations/messages schema already supported the flow.

content:
Summary: Adjusted the chat UI layout so the command center message list scrolls independently and the composer stays attached to the bottom without a separator line. The change was made primarily in the CommandCenter component, with a small parent layout tweak in the page to ensure the flex column can shrink correctly.

Decisions:
1. [finished] Keep the layout fix inside the existing CommandCenter component instead of extracting a new chat layout module, because the change is narrowly scoped to panel composition and scroll behavior.
2. [finished] Use flex column sizing with an internal scroll container rather than position: sticky/fixed for the composer, preserving the existing panel shell behavior while making the message list independently scrollable.

Affected files:
1. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\canvas-ai\src\components\CommandCenter.tsx — Contains the message list and composer layout; updated to make the message list scrollable and remove the separator above the composer. | Notes: Added flex sizing and overflow handling to the message list wrapper and removed the composer top border.
2. [modified] C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\canvas-ai\src\app\page.tsx — Provides the chat panel container whose flex sizing must allow the internal scroll area to shrink properly. | Notes: Added min-h-0 to the chat panel wrapper and its sibling split panel wrapper.

metadata.summary: Adjusted the chat UI layout so the command center message list scrolls independently and the composer stays attached to the bottom without a separator line. The change was made primarily in the CommandCenter component, with a small parent layout tweak in the page to ensure the flex column can shrink correctly.

content:
Summary: Implemented a shared workspace settings system so the settings modal now controls real UI behavior across the app. The settings state was lifted to the page and threaded into the sidebar, artifact viewer, command center, and modal. Theme now updates the overall shell and panel styling, density changes spacing, font size adjusts artifact text/table rendering, line numbers can be toggled in the viewer, and auto-scroll can be disabled in the chat panel. The settings modal was also kept as a dedicated overlay component, with the About section removed earlier and the modal now reflecting the shared theme state.

Decisions:
1. [finished] Keep settings UI as a modal overlay component while moving the actual setting values to page-level shared state so multiple panels can respond consistently.
2. [finished] Use a small shared settings contract module instead of duplicating setting types across components.
3. [finished] Keep the modal content presentational and let the page own state mutation so future settings can be wired in one place.

Affected files:
1. [modified] src/app/page.tsx — Lifted settings state to the workspace page and passed shared settings into the sidebar, artifact viewer, and command center. | Notes: Also updated the mobile shell to reflect theme changes.
2. [modified] src/components/SettingsModal.tsx — Converted modal controls to receive shared settings state and dispatch updates instead of maintaining isolated local state. | Notes: The modal now reflects the app theme and density state.
3. [modified] src/components/Sidebar.tsx — Sidebar now passes the shared settings state into the modal so theme changes and control updates can affect the whole app. | Notes: The settings button still opens the modal.
4. [modified] src/components/ArtifactViewer.tsx — Artifact rendering now responds to shared line-number, font-size, theme, and density settings. | Notes: Text and sheet rendering both use the shared settings contract.
5. [modified] src/components/CommandCenter.tsx — Chat panel now respects shared theme, density, and auto-scroll settings. | Notes: Auto-scroll can be toggled off from the modal.
6. [created] src/components/workspaceSettings.ts — Introduced a shared typed settings contract to avoid duplicating setting shapes and to keep state updates explicit. | Notes: Defines the default workspace settings and updater type.

The settings modal was also kept as a dedicated overlay component, with the About section removed earlier and the modal now reflecting the shared theme state.
